source_item.title: ICICI Bank Q1 Results: Profit jumps 16% YoY to Rs 14,805 crore; NII rises 13% as margins expand

slide1_hook.label: ICICI Bank's Biggest Profit Quarter Ever
slide1_hook.subhead: ₹14,805 crore in one quarter, and margins are rising.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From World Bank Project to Profit Machine
slide2_backstory.body: ICICI started in 1955 as a World Bank-backed lender for India's industry. It's now India's second-largest bank by assets, after becoming a private bank in 1994.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Q1 FY27: 16% Profit Jump, Margins Expand
slide3_news.body: ICICI Bank's profit jumped 16% to ₹14,805 crore this quarter. Its lending grew too, with advances up 19.6% to ₹16.3 lakh crore.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Why India Should Care About This Number
bullets: Margins rising even as RBI cuts rates
Bad loans falling, credit quality improving
₹22,963 crore set aside as a safety buffer

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: ICICI is beating HDFC Bank on profit growth this quarter. Is India's private banking crown about to change hands?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily Indian business stories that actually matter. Drop your take in the comments 👇
